title: navarretia intertexta subsp. propinqua
intro: Navarretia intertexta subsp. propinqua, also known as the coastal bluebells or western bluebells, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the phlox family (Polemoniaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Navarretia intertexta subsp. propinqua include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
